commit.message:
fix: use full ARN URL + classic Cognito flow for HTTP chat invocations

AgentCore /invocations endpoint requires the full URL-encoded runtime ARN
in the path — the short ID returns HTTP 400 with:
  'accountID is required when agentRuntimeArn is provided as agentId'

The Cognito enhanced authflow session policy also blocks InvokeAgentRuntime
(same root cause as WebSocket). Classic flow works for both.

Changes:
- agentcoreClient.js: getAwsCredentials() now supports classic Cognito flow
  via optional {identityPoolId, region, unauthRoleArn} params; signedFetch()
  accepts cognitoOpts as 5th param and passes them through
- useChat.js: always sign AgentCore requests (not only when authenticated);
  pass identityPoolId + unauthRoleArn for classic flow; remove dead else branch
- deploy.sh: chat_url + voice_ws_url now use URL-encoded full ARN
- .env.local (local): updated VITE_AGENTCORE_CHAT_URL to use full ARN URL

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: use classic Cognito authflow for WebSocket presigning

Cognito's enhanced authflow (GetCredentialsForIdentity) attaches an
internal session policy that does NOT include the newer
bedrock-agentcore:InvokeAgentRuntimeWithWebSocketStream action, causing
all WebSocket connections from the browser to return HTTP 403.

Root cause: AWS Cognito's enhanced authflow passes a Policy document
when calling AssumeRoleWithWebIdentity internally. That session policy
does not list the WebSocket action, so IAM denies it even though the
role's identity-based policy explicitly allows it.

Fix: implement the classic Cognito authflow for WebSocket URL presigning:
  GetId → GetOpenIdToken → AssumeRoleWithWebIdentity (no Policy param)
This gives pure role-policy credentials with no session-policy restriction.

The enhanced flow is kept for HTTP signedFetch (chat works fine there).

Changes:
- agentcoreClient.js: add getCognitoClassicCredentials() using
  @aws-sdk/client-cognito-identity + @aws-sdk/credential-providers
  fromWebToken; createPresignedWebSocketUrl accepts identityPoolId +
  unauthRoleArn params and uses classic flow when both are present
- useConnection.js: add cognitoUnauthRoleArn field (VITE_COGNITO_UNAUTH_ROLE_ARN)
- useVoice.js: pass identityPoolId + unauthRoleArn to presign function
- scripts/deploy.sh: write VITE_COGNITO_UNAUTH_ROLE_ARN to .env.local;
  fix server_protocol from invalid HTTP_AND_WEBSOCKET back to HTTP
  (HTTP_AND_WEBSOCKET is not a valid enum; WebSocket is always available)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: create AgentCore runtime with HTTP_AND_WEBSOCKET protocol (#11)

Without this, the runtime is created with serverProtocol=HTTP which means
WebSocket connections are rejected with HTTP 403 regardless of IAM policy.

The protocolConfiguration.serverProtocol must be HTTP_AND_WEBSOCKET for
the /ws endpoint to accept WebSocket upgrade requests.

Also patched the live runtime via update_agent_runtime() directly.

Co-authored-by: FlutterOTA Setup <flutterota@example.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: use full URL-encoded runtime ARN for AgentCore WebSocket presigning (#10)

Root cause: the AgentCore /ws endpoint requires the full runtime ARN
(URL-encoded) in the path, not the short runtime ID:

  WRONG: /runtimes/aria_banking_agent-ubLoKG8xsY/ws → HTTP 403
  RIGHT: /runtimes/arn%3Aaws%3Abedrock-agentcore%3A.../ws → HTTP 101

This mirrors how the official AgentCore Python SDK builds presigned URLs.

Additional corrections to match SDK behaviour:
- Sign as https:// (not wss://) before converting back to wss://
- Add X-Amzn-Bedrock-AgentCore-Runtime-Session-Id query param (UUID per session)
- Reduce expiresIn from 3600 to 300 (SDK default is 300s)

New env var VITE_AGENTCORE_RUNTIME_ARN added:
- deploy.sh writes full ARN to client/.env.local
- useConnection.js reads VITE_AGENTCORE_RUNTIME_ARN into agentcoreRuntimeArn
- useVoice.js passes config.agentcoreRuntimeArn to createPresignedWebSocketUrl

Co-authored-by: FlutterOTA Setup <flutterota@example.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: add InvokeAgentRuntimeWithWebSocketStream IAM action + remove voice auth gate (#9)

Root cause of 'WebSocket connection error':
The Cognito unauthenticated role was missing the IAM action required for
AgentCore WebSocket connections. The /ws endpoint requires a separate action:
  bedrock-agentcore:InvokeAgentRuntimeWithWebSocketStream
Without it, the presigned WebSocket upgrade request returns HTTP 403, which
the browser translates to ws.onerror with no status code exposed to JS.

Changes:
1. scripts/deploy.sh — add InvokeAgentRuntimeWithWebSocketStream to the
   Cognito unauth IAM policy (alongside the existing InvokeAgentRuntime actions)

2. client/src/hooks/useVoice.js — remove incorrect 'authenticated' guard on
   AgentCore voice. The banking-level auth flag is already sent to the backend
   in session.config (ws.onopen), so voice should work for both authenticated
   and unauthenticated banking sessions. Guard is replaced with a check for
   cognitoIdentityPoolId (actual AWS auth requirement).

3. client/src/hooks/useVoice.js — improve ws.onerror message in AgentCore mode
   to mention the specific IAM action and Cognito pool as things to check.

Note: the live IAM policy has already been patched via aws iam put-role-policy.

Co-authored-by: FlutterOTA Setup <flutterota@example.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: upgrade @vitejs/plugin-react to v6 to support vite 8 (#8)

vite@8.0.3 was installed but @vitejs/plugin-react@4.x only supports
vite ^4-7, causing ERESOLVE peer dependency conflict on npm install.

@vitejs/plugin-react@6.0.1 declares peer vite@^8.0.0 — bump to align.
The plugin API (react() call in vite.config.js) is unchanged between v4 and v6.

Co-authored-by: FlutterOTA Setup <flutterota@example.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: show npm output during deploy so terminal doesn't appear frozen (#7)

Previously 'npm ci --silent 2>/dev/null || npm install --silent' produced
zero output, making the deploy script appear hung during package installation
(which can take several minutes on a cold run).

Changes:
- Remove --silent / 2>/dev/null from both npm commands
- Add --no-audit --no-fund to skip slow network checks
- Use if/else instead of || so npm ci exit code is handled correctly
- Capture build output separately (local + assignment on same line swallows
  exit codes in bash 3/4); check exit code explicitly
- Show 'first run may take a few minutes' hint in the step message

Co-authored-by: FlutterOTA Setup <flutterota@example.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: default to agentcore mode when deployed to CloudFront (#6)

When VITE_AGENTCORE_CHAT_URL is present in the build (i.e. when deploy.sh
builds the React app and writes .env.local before bundling), the app now
defaults to agentcore mode automatically.

Without this fix, the CloudFront-hosted app defaults to local mode and
tries to reach http://localhost:8080 from an HTTPS page — blocked by
browser mixed-content rules.

Co-authored-by: FlutterOTA Setup <flutterota@example.com>

feat: deploy React app to S3 + CloudFront in agentcore deploy

## What's added
- create_react_client_bucket(): private S3 bucket (meridian-aria-client-<id>)
  for React static files; public access blocked, served via CloudFront OAC
- create_cloudfront_distribution(): global CloudFront distribution with:
  - Origin Access Control (OAC) — no public S3 URLs
  - S3 regional origin (OAC-compatible virtual-hosted endpoint)
  - HTTPS redirect, HTTP/2+3, PriceClass_100
  - Custom error: 403/404 → index.html (SPA routing)
  - CachingOptimized policy for assets; no-cache on index.html
- build_and_deploy_react(): npm ci + npm run build + aws s3 sync
  - Hashed assets: max-age=31536000,immutable
  - index.html: no-cache,no-store (always fresh)
  - CloudFront invalidation /* after every deploy
- write_react_env(): adds VITE_CLOUDFRONT_URL to client/.env.local
- print_summary(): prominently displays CloudFront URL + distribution ID
  and includes one-liner re-deploy commands for client-only updates
- cmd_status(): shows CloudFront URL, distribution ID and S3 bucket
- cmd_teardown(): disables distribution, waits for Deployed state,
  deletes distribution + OAC + empties/deletes client S3 bucket

## Deploy order (agentcore target)
  ... existing steps ...
  deploy_cognito_identity_pool
  create_react_client_bucket       ← new
  create_cloudfront_distribution   ← new (sets CF_DOMAIN in state before build)
  write_react_env                  ← updated (adds VITE_CLOUDFRONT_URL)
  build_and_deploy_react           ← new
  print_summary                    ← updated (shows app URL prominently)

commit.message:
Switch layout updates to UpdateLayout message

* Use structured Measure/Arrange message args

Replace the loose Size/rect message pointer types with explicit MeasureMsgArgs and ArrangeMsgArgs structs across UIKit. Update code to use capitalized fields (Width/Height/X/Y) and to call _SendMessage(...) with the new argument syntax instead of older OBJ_SendMessageW usages. Adjust all MAKEDWORD/measure/arrange accesses to use pMeasureOverride->Width/Height and pArrangeOverride->X/Y where applicable. Update UIKit.xml to define Measure/Arrange message fields and inherit MeasureOverride/ArrangeOverride, and add corresponding Lua bindings and metatables for MeasureMsgArgs and ArrangeMsgArgs in UIKit_export.c. This centralizes message argument layout and exposes the new types to Lua.

* Enable `_SendMessage` with `kMsgHitTest` and `kMsgForegroundContent` via typed struct fields (#230)

* Initial plan

* Enable _SendMessage with kMsgHitTest by adding typed struct fields

Co-authored-by: corepunch <83646194+corepunch@users.noreply.github.com>

* Use _SendMessage and adjust message bindings

Replace direct OBJ_SendMessageW calls with the internal _SendMessage API across UIKit and object code (Screen.c, UIKit.c, object_lua.c) and pass explicit x/y for HitTest. Update HitTestMsgArgs setter to use luaL_optinteger with a default of 0. Move PropertyChangedMsgArgs declaration in core.h and relocate its Lua binding implementations in core_export.c, adjusting the registration order in luaopen_orca_core. These changes unify the messaging calls, provide safer defaults for hit-test fields, and tidy up Lua type bindings.

* Switch layout updates to UpdateLayout message

Replace direct OBJ_UpdateLayout calls with a message-based UpdateLayout flow. Adds an UpdateLayout message and UpdateLayoutMsgArgs (XML, headers, and Lua bindings), implements Screen handler for UpdateLayout, and updates callers (UIKit Screen, editor canvas, docs and tests) to use sendMessage2/_SendMessage. Removes the old public OBJ_UpdateLayout API and related export, and updates core docs accordingly. This centralizes layout work into the object message system and exposes it to Lua.

* Remove duplicate message structs and bindings

Eliminates redundant definitions and registrations for HitTestMsgArgs and PropertyChangedMsgArgs. The duplicate struct declarations and luaX push/check prototypes were removed from core.h, and the duplicated lua_setfield calls were removed from core_export.c to avoid redefinition and duplicate Lua registrations.

commit.message:
Feature/more send message (#231)

* Use structured Measure/Arrange message args

Replace the loose Size/rect message pointer types with explicit MeasureMsgArgs and ArrangeMsgArgs structs across UIKit. Update code to use capitalized fields (Width/Height/X/Y) and to call _SendMessage(...) with the new argument syntax instead of older OBJ_SendMessageW usages. Adjust all MAKEDWORD/measure/arrange accesses to use pMeasureOverride->Width/Height and pArrangeOverride->X/Y where applicable. Update UIKit.xml to define Measure/Arrange message fields and inherit MeasureOverride/ArrangeOverride, and add corresponding Lua bindings and metatables for MeasureMsgArgs and ArrangeMsgArgs in UIKit_export.c. This centralizes message argument layout and exposes the new types to Lua.

* Normalize message typedefs and Width/Height fields

Standardize message typedef naming and field access across the UI/core layers.

- Replace pointer-only typedefs with paired _t and pointer typedefs (e.g. MeasureMsg_t, MeasureMsgPtr) in UIKit, core, filesystem and renderer headers.
- Update message construction macro to use MESSAGE##Msg_t instead of struct MESSAGE##MsgArgs in include/orcadef.h.
- Update code to use the new message types and the camel-cased Size/Arrange fields (Width/Height, X/Y) in Grid, Node2D, StackView handlers to match the new types.
- Update codegen template (tools/templates/header.php) to emit the new typedef pattern so generated headers match the code changes.

These changes unify type naming, fix struct literal casts, and ensure generated headers and call sites use consistent message types and field names.

* Expose WindowPaint args and Lua bindings

Add explicit WindowPaintMsgArgs structure and alias WindowResizedMsg_t, and extend core message metadata to include WindowWidth/WindowHeight (WindowResized now derives from WindowPaint) in core.xml. Implement Lua userdata bindings for WindowPaintMsgArgs (push/check, constructor, __index/__newindex, and registration in luaopen_orca_core) in core_export.c so paint/resized message data can be accessed from Lua. Also reorder a message ID define in core_properties.h to keep constants consistent with the message changes.

* Update StackView.c

* Removed more obj_sendmessagew

* Replace OBJ_SendMessageW with _SendMessage in Trigger, Screen, property_events (#228)

* Initial plan

commit.message:
perf: critical Core Web Vitals fixes — target 60-70% improvement

- Defer Three.js loading 3.5s after page load (unblocks FCP/LCP)
- Change audio preload='auto' → 'none' (saves 4.4MB initial download)
- Parallelize DB queries with Promise.all (cuts TTFB ~400-800ms)
- Remove 'unoptimized' from Images (enables Next.js WebP/AVIF optimization)
- Reduce ISR cache from 1hr to 5min for fresher content
- Trim Google font weights (remove unused 300 weight, add display:swap)
- Preload critical Styrene font, change to font-display:optional (CLS fix)
- Lazy-load typewriter audio only when unmuted
- Remove unused space.wav (27MB) and space.ogg (1.6MB) from public/

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: comprehensive admin UX improvements

- Unsaved changes tracking with dirty state per tab + beforeunload warning
- Toast system with undo support and stacked container
- ProjectsTab: search/filter, reorder, tech chips, inline delete confirm, empty state, validation
- SkillsTab: search/filter, reorder, inline delete confirm, empty state, validation

commit.message:
feat: always-on voice, barge-in, session fixes & layout ## Voice UX (always-on, no push-to-talk) - Replace push-to-talk with always-on microphone that activates on connect - VAD-based barge-in: RMS > 600 threshold, 1s cooldown between interrupts - Track ARIA-speaking state via 600ms debounce; send {interrupted:true} to server - audioPlayer.bargeIn() stops all queued audio and resets playback schedule - Server already handled interrupt messages; client now correctly triggers them ## VoiceTab UI redesign - Animated orb: pulsing green = listening, blue ripple = ARIA speaking, spin = connecting - Chat bubble layout: user bubbles right/blue, ARIA bubbles left/dark - Waveform canvas strip showing live audio activity - Status bar shows mic/speaking state; no push-to-talk button - Newline characters in ARIA responses rendered as separate

bubbles - Transcript auto-scrolls to bottom on new messages ## Layout fix (header no longer scrolls off-screen) - Add fitHeight + className='app-tabs' to Cloudscape in App.jsx - Only .voice-messages scrolls; header bar + tab strip always stay visible ## Nova Sonic session reliability - Fix critical bug: is_active never set True — all while-loops exited immediately causing instant WS disconnect after connect - Transient errors (System instability detected, throttling, 600s limit) now trigger silent stream renewal instead of terminating the session - _session_watchdog renews at 560s completely silently — no user-visible messages - History injected as AWS-style role-tagged content blocks on renewal ## Auth stale-closure fix - configRef = useRef(config) + useEffect to keep ref current - ws.onopen reads configRef.current (always latest) not closure-captured config - Ensures authenticated=true and customer_id reach Nova Sonic on session start ## File logging - _configure_logging() in agentcore_app.py: RotatingFileHandler (aria_agentcore.log, 10MB x5) + StreamHandler; called at import so all aria.* loggers get handlers - All exception handlers in run() log with exc_info=True Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: add CORS middleware so React dev server can connect to local WebSocket

The React dev server runs on http://localhost:5173, the ARIA backend on
ws://localhost:8080/ws. Without CORS headers the browser silently drops
the WebSocket upgrade (connection stays in 'connecting' state forever).

- Add CORSMiddleware(allow_origins=['*']) to BedrockAgentCoreApp
- This only affects local uvicorn — in AgentCore production the runtime
  proxy handles auth/routing before our app code is reached

feat: add React client for ARIA chat and voice

Complete Vite + React 18 client application for the ARIA banking agent
(Meridian Bank) running on Amazon Bedrock AgentCore.

## Features
- Chat tab: HTTP POST /invocations with message bubbles, typing indicator,
  session ID, clear conversation
- Voice tab: WebSocket bidirectional audio with Nova Sonic S2S protocol,
  waveform visualiser, transcript display, mic button
- Connection toggle: Local (localhost:8080) ↔ AgentCore runtime endpoint
- Customer ID input (default CUST-001) sent on every request
- Auth toggle: unauthenticated / authenticated (SigV4 signing via
  @aws-sdk/signature-v4 + @aws-crypto/sha256-js for browser compat)
- CollapsibleConnection Settings panel: URLs, AWS credentials, region
- All config persisted to localStorage

## Tech stack
- Vite 5 + React 18
- @cloudscape-design/components (Meridian Bank teal/navy theme)
- Real PCM audio capture: ScriptProcessorNode, linear resampling → 16 kHz
- Gapless PCM playback: AudioContext at 24 kHz, queued BufferSource nodes
- uuid v9 for session IDs

commit.message:
fix: IAM propagation delay on CodeBuild project creation

When SDK deletes and recreates the CodeBuild execution role between
failed runs, CreateProject immediately fails because IAM takes 10-30s
to propagate trust policies.

Fixes:
- patch_agentcore_yaml(): verify each stored ARN actually exists in AWS
  via 'aws iam get-role' / 'aws ecr describe-repositories' before trusting
  it. Stale ARNs (role was deleted) are set to null so SDK auto-creates
  fresh resources instead of using a ghost reference.
- launch_agentcore(): retry loop (3 attempts, 30s wait between) that
  detects IAM trust policy errors in the output and retries. Also
  regenerates the YAML before each retry (SDK deletes it on failure).
- State file: clear stale CodeBuild/ECR ARNs from previous failed run.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: self-healing YAML regeneration + restore deleted config

The SDK deletes .bedrock_agentcore.yaml on failed runs, breaking
subsequent deploys with FileNotFoundError.

Fix: rewrite patch_agentcore_yaml() to always regenerate the YAML
from scratch using Python yaml.dump, pulling values from:
  1. State file (scripts/.deploy-state.json) — populated by deploy
  2. Existing YAML (if present) — preserves SDK-populated ARNs
  3. Script variables (AGENTCORE_REGION, ACCOUNT_ID, AGENT_NAME)

This means the YAML is always correct at deploy time regardless of
whether the SDK deleted or corrupted it in a previous run.

Also:
- Store SDK-populated values in state file (execution_role_arn,
  ecr_repository, codebuild_project_name, etc.) so regeneration
  works even on a fresh checkout
- Restore .bedrock_agentcore.yaml with all correct resource ARNs
